title: श्राद्ध मन्त्र
devanagariText:
॥ श्री श्राद्ध मन्त्र संग्रह ॥
॥ पितृ तर्पण एवं श्राद्ध विधि ॥

॥ गणेश वन्दना ॥

ॐ गं गणपतये नमः।

॥ पितृ ध्यान मन्त्र ॥

ॐ पितृभ्यः स्वधायिभ्यः स्वधा नमः।
पितामहेभ्यः स्वधायिभ्यः स्वधा नमः।
प्रपितामहेभ्यः स्वधायिभ्यः स्वधा नमः॥

पिता, पितामह (दादा), प्रपितामह (परदादा) — तीनों पीढ़ियों के पितरों को स्वधा (श्रद्धापूर्वक) नमस्कार।

॥ संकल्प मन्त्र ॥

ॐ विष्णुर्विष्णुर्विष्णुः। ॐ तत्सत्।
अमुकगोत्रस्य अमुकशर्मणः पितुः/मातुः
वार्षिकश्राद्धं/तिथिश्राद्धं अहं करिष्ये।
तदंगभूत-पितृतर्पणं च करिष्ये।

॥ तिलतर्पण मन्त्र ॥

(दक्षिण दिशा की ओर मुख करके, यज्ञोपवीत अपसव्य — दाहिने कन्धे पर)

ॐ अमुकगोत्रान् अमुकशर्मणः पितॄन् स्वधा नमः तर्पयामि।
ॐ अमुकगोत्रान् अमुकशर्मणः पितामहान् स्वधा नमः तर्पयामि।
ॐ अमुकगोत्रान् अमुकशर्मणः प्रपितामहान् स्वधा नमः तर्पयामि।

(तिल-जल-अक्षत मिलाकर तीन-तीन अञ्जलि प्रत्येक पीढ़ी को)

॥ मातृ पक्ष तर्पण ॥

ॐ अमुकगोत्रान् अमुकनाम्नीम् मातरम् स्वधा नमः तर्पयामि।
ॐ अमुकगोत्रान् अमुकनाम्नीम् पितामहीम् स्वधा नमः तर्पयामि।
ॐ अमुकगोत्रान् अमुकनाम्नीम् प्रपितामहीम् स्वधा नमः तर्पयामि।

॥ पिण्डदान मन्त्र ॥

ॐ ये चेह पितरो ये च नेह यांश्च विद्म यांश्चु न प्रविद्म।
त्वं वेत्थ यति ते जातवेदः ताभ्यः स्वधा अनु ये तृप्यन्तु॥

हे अग्निदेव! जो पितर यहाँ हैं और जो नहीं हैं, जिन्हें हम जानते हैं और जिन्हें नहीं जानते — आप जानते हैं कि वे कितने हैं। उन सबको स्वधा से तृप्त करें।

(चावल, तिल, जौ का पिण्ड बनाकर कुश पर रखें)

॥ विष्णु पितृ मन्त्र ॥

ॐ नमो वः पितरो रसाय
नमो वः पितरः शोषाय
नमो वः पितरो जीवाय
नमो वः पितरः स्वधायै
नमो वः पितरो मन्यवे
नमो वः पितरो घोराय॥

हे पितरो! रस को नमस्कार, शोष को नमस्कार, जीवन को नमस्कार, स्वधा को नमस्कार, मन्यु को नमस्कार, घोर को नमस्कार।

॥ पितृ गायत्री मन्त्र ॥

ॐ पितृभ्यो विद्महे जगतपालकेभ्यो धीमहि।
तन्नः पितरः प्रचोदयात्॥

॥ श्राद्ध भोजन मन्त्र ॥

ॐ अन्नं न परित्यक्तव्यम्। तद्‍व्रतम्।
आपो वा अन्नम्। ज्योतिर्वा अन्नम्।

ॐ अन्नपतेऽन्नस्य नो देह्यनमीवस्य शुष्मिणः।
प्र प्रदातारं तारिषत् ऊर्जं नो धेहि द्विपदे चतुष्पदे॥

(ब्राह्मण भोजन कराकर दक्षिणा दें — वस्त्र, अन्न, धन सहित)

॥ पितृ विसर्जन मन्त्र ॥

ॐ ऊर्जं वहन्तीरमृतं घृतं पयः
कीलालं परिस्रुतम्।
स्वधा स्थ तर्पयत मे पितॄन्॥

हे अमृत, घृत, दुग्ध, मधु को वहन करने वाली (आहुतियाँ)! आप स्वधा हैं — मेरे पितरों को तृप्त करें।

त्रिपदे पूर्णं पात्रमानयत पिण्डान् निर्वपत।
पितरः सम्प्रीयन्तां। गच्छध्वं पितरः स्वधां भक्तवन्तः॥

हे पितरो! स्वधा भोग करके प्रसन्न हों, तृप्त हों, कल्याण मार्ग पर जायें।

॥ श्राद्ध सम्पन्न प्रार्थना ॥

ॐ मधु वाता ऋतायते मधु क्षरन्ति सिन्धवः।
माध्वीर्नः सन्त्वोषधीः॥

पितरों को नमस्कार। उनकी कृपा से कुल में सुख-शान्ति-समृद्धि बनी रहे। पितृ ऋण से मुक्ति हो।

॥ इति श्री श्राद्ध मन्त्र संग्रह सम्पूर्ण ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Shraddha Mantra Sangraha ||

|| Pitru Dhyana Mantra ||
Om Pitribhyah Svadhayibhyah Svadha Namah.
Pitamahebhyah Svadhayibhyah Svadha Namah.
Prapitamahebhyah Svadhayibhyah Svadha Namah.

|| Tila-Tarpana Mantra ||
Om (Gotra) (Nama) Pitrin Svadha Namah Tarpayami.
Om (Gotra) (Nama) Pitamahan Svadha Namah Tarpayami.
Om (Gotra) (Nama) Prapitamahan Svadha Namah Tarpayami.

|| Matru Paksha Tarpana ||
Om (Gotra) (Nama) Mataram Svadha Namah Tarpayami.

|| Pindadana Mantra ||
Om Ye Cheha Pitaro Ye Cha Neha
Yanshcha Vidma Yansh-chu Na Pravidma.
Tvam Vettha Yati Te Jatavedah
Tabhyah Svadha Anu Ye Tripyantu.

|| Vishnu Pitru Mantra ||
Om Namo Vah Pitaro Rasaya...

|| Pitru Gayatri ||
Om Pitribhyo Vidmahe Jagat-palakebhyo Dhimahi.
Tannah Pitarah Prachodayat.

|| Shraddha Bhojana ||
Om Annapateh Annasya No Dehi.

|| Pitru Visarjana ||
Om Urjam Vahantir-amritam Ghritam Payah.
Svadha Stha Tarpayata Me Pitrin.

|| Iti Shri Shraddha Mantra Sangraha Sampurna ||
hindiMeaning: श्राद्ध मन्त्र संग्रह — पितृ तर्पण और श्राद्ध विधि। पितृ ध्यान — तीन पीढ़ियों (पिता, पितामह, प्रपितामह) और मातृ पक्ष को स्वधा-नमस्कार। तिल-तर्पण — दक्षिण दिशा में अपसव्य होकर तिल-जल-अक्षत से तीन-तीन अंजलि। पिण्डदान — चावल-तिल-जौ का पिण्ड कुश पर रखकर अर्पण, अग्निदेव से प्रार्थना कि ज्ञात-अज्ञात सभी पितरों को तृप्त करें। पितृ गायत्री — जगत्पालक पितरों का ध्यान। ब्राह्मण भोजन, दक्षिणा। पितृ विसर्जन — अमृत-घृत-दुग्ध से पितरों को तृप्ति, कल्याण मार्ग पर विदा। श्राद्ध से पितृ ऋण मुक्ति, कुल में सुख-शान्ति-समृद्धि।
benefits: Complete mantra collection for performing Shraddha (ancestral rites) and Pitru Tarpana
Liberates ancestors and frees the performer from Pitru Dosha
Includes tarpana mantras for paternal and maternal lineages (three generations each)
Contains authentic Pindadana mantra from Vedic tradition
Essential for Pitru Paksha, annual death anniversary (tithi), and Amavasya shraddha
Traditionally associated with peace to departed souls and prosperity to the living family
description: The Shraddha Mantras, known in Sanskrit as श्राद्ध मन्त्र, is a sacred prayer in praise of Lord Vishnu. This revered text originates from Grihya Sutras, Garuda Purana, Vayu Purana. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to contain authentic Pindadana mantra from Vedic tradition as well as bring peace to departed souls and prosperity to the living family. Additionally, it is known to complete mantra collection for performing Shraddha (ancestral rites) and Pitru Tarpana. This sacred text is ideally recited on Saturday (Shanivar) and during Pitru Paksha for enhanced blessings. At 18 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 12 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Shraddha Mantras in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Shraddha Mantras in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Complete Pitru Tarpana and Pindadana mantras for ancestral rites.
source: Grihya Sutras, Garuda Purana, Vayu Purana
relatedSearches: Shraddha mantras for Pitru Paksha lyrics in Sanskrit
Pitru tarpana mantra PDF Hindi meaning download
Shraddha mantra for father mother grandfather
Tila tarpana mantra with sesame seeds water

seoFaqs.question: What are the Shraddha Mantras and when are they used?
seoFaqs.answer: The Shraddha Mantras are the collection of Sanskrit invocations used during the Hindu rite of Shraddha — the post-funerary ceremony of offering food, water and respect to the departed ancestors (pitrs). They are recited principally on the tithi of a parent's or ancestor's death (mrityu-tithi), during the annual Pitru Paksha fortnight in Bhadrapada krishna paksha culminating in Mahalaya Amavasya, and at major life events such as weddings (Nandi Shraddha) and pilgrimages to Gaya, Prayagraj and Kashi. The mantras combine Vedic verses from the Rigveda Pitru Sukta and Yajurveda Kanda 2 with paddhati prescriptions from Apastamba, Hiranyakeshi, Bodhayana and other Grihya Sutra families, and puranic verses from Garuda Purana and Vayu Purana. (Sources: Hindupedia Shraddha; Vedadhara; Drikpanchang Pitru Paksha.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the main scriptural sources of the Shraddha Mantras?
seoFaqs.answer: The Vedic foundation lies in the Rigveda Pitru Sukta (10.15) addressed to the ancestors, the Yajurveda mantras for pinda offering, and the Atharvaveda hymns on ancestor welfare. The procedural framework comes from the Grihya Sutras of Apastamba, Hiranyakeshi, Ashvalayana, Paraskara and Bodhayana, each lineage maintaining its own paddhati for shraddha karma. Puranic elaboration appears in the Garuda Purana (especially the Pretakhanda dealing with post-death rites), Vayu Purana, Markandeya Purana, and the Pitru Mahatmya sections of various puranas. The Manusmriti, Yajnavalkya Smriti and Parashara Smriti provide the dharmashastra rules for who is eligible to perform shraddha and on which tithis. (Sources: Hindupedia Grihya Sutras; Vedadhara; Sanskritdocuments.org.)

seoFaqs.question: When is Pitru Paksha and what role do these mantras play?
seoFaqs.answer: Pitru Paksha is the fortnight of the dark half of Bhadrapada (September-October in the Gregorian calendar), beginning the day after Bhadrapada Purnima and ending on Sarva Pitru Amavasya, also called Mahalaya Amavasya. During these fifteen tithis the ancestors are believed to descend to earth to receive the offerings of their descendants, and each tithi is reserved for relatives who departed on that lunar day. The Shraddha Mantras are recited daily during this period for the relevant tithi's ancestors, with a special concentrated effort on Mahalaya Amavasya for all departed kin together. Performing shraddha during this fortnight is considered to release pitru-rina (debt to ancestors) and prevent pitru-dosha. (Sources: Drikpanchang Pitru Paksha; Hindupedia Mahalaya; Vedadhara.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the essential elements of a Shraddha ceremony?
seoFaqs.answer: A traditional shraddha typically includes four essential components, each accompanied by specific mantras. First, tarpana — offering water mixed with black sesame (tila) and barley to the ancestors using the pitru-tirtha mudra (water flowing past the thumb), accompanied by Pitru Sukta and tarpana mantras. Second, pinda-dana — offering rice balls (pindas) made of cooked rice, sesame, ghee, honey and milk to the named ancestors, with mantras invoking the father, grandfather, great-grandfather and corresponding maternal lines. Third, brahmana-bhojana — feeding qualified brahmins or substituting cooked food offered to crows, cows and dogs (representing yama-dutas and ancestor envoys). Fourth, dakshina and dana — offering gifts and donations as conclusion. (Sources: Hindupedia Shraddha; Vayu Purana; Garuda Purana Pretakhanda.)

seoFaqs.question: Can women perform shraddha and recite these mantras?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditionally shraddha karma was performed by the eldest son or, in his absence, by other male descendants in a defined order — younger sons, grandsons, nephews and so on. However, classical dharmashastra texts including Manusmriti and later commentaries acknowledge that in the absence of male descendants the daughter, daughter's son, or even a wife can perform shraddha; modern paddhatis cited by Hindupedia and Sanatan Sanstha confirm this. In contemporary practice across India many women now perform shraddha for their parents, particularly when there is no surviving son, and major shraddha-tirthas like Gaya, Prayagraj and Kashi accept rituals performed by daughters. The mantras themselves are not gender-restricted Vedic mantras but smriti-paddhati invocations open to any qualified devotee. (Sources: Hindupedia Shraddha; Sanatan Sanstha; Drikpanchang.)

seoFaqs.question: What is Pitru Dosha and how do Shraddha Mantras help?
seoFaqs.answer: Pitru Dosha is a karmic affliction said to arise when the ancestors are not properly honoured through annual shraddha or when there is unresolved generational karma in the family — manifesting in jyotisha through the Sun-Saturn or Sun-Rahu combinations and in life as recurrent obstacles to marriage, progeny, prosperity or health. The traditional remedy is to perform shraddha consistently on the relevant tithis during Pitru Paksha, with optional pilgrimages to Gaya for the Vishnupada Gaya-shraddha which is said to grant moksha to all ancestors up to seven generations. The Shraddha Mantras invoke the ancestors directly by name, gotra and lineage, repaying pitru-rina and gradually dissolving the dosha. (Sources: Hindupedia Pitru Dosha; Garuda Purana; Drikpanchang.)

seoFaqs.question: Can Shraddha Mantras be recited at home without a priest?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes, the basic tarpana — offering water with sesame to the ancestors — can be performed at home daily after the morning bath, especially during Pitru Paksha, by any householder who has been initiated into the basic mantras through family tradition or a qualified guru. The simpler home tarpana uses the Pitru Sukta and the names of one's immediate forebears, takes only ten to fifteen minutes, and is highly recommended even when a full pandit-led shraddha is not possible. The full shraddha karma — with pinda-dana, brahmana-bhojana, agni-mukha rites and complete Vedic mantras — does require an experienced purohit familiar with the family's grihya-sutra lineage to ensure correct procedural fidelity. (Sources: Hindupedia; Sanatan Sanstha; Drikpanchang.)
